description_long: This object is a realistic representation of a freshwater crustacean, likely a crayfish or small lobster, with a robust, segmented body and a hard exoskeleton. The cephalothorax is broad and slightly arched, featuring two prominent claws (chelae) of unequal size, and several pairs of walking legs. The abdomen is elongated, segmented, and tapers towards a fan-shaped tail, with visible swimmerets underneath. Two long, slender antennae extend from the head, accompanied by smaller antennules. The eyes are positioned on short stalks at the front of the cephalothorax, providing a wide field of vision. The coloration is predominantly dark brown with subtle greenish and reddish hues, and the surface appears hard and slightly glossy, typical of a living or freshly caught crustacean.
description: A detailed, dark brown crustacean with segmented body, large claws, long antennae, and a hard, glossy exoskeleton.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the body, both claws, and the extended antennae.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the segmentation of the abdomen, the symmetry of the legs, and the spread of the tail.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other profile, with the claws and antennae visible, and the body curvature evident.
description_view_3: Frontal view emphasizing the large claws, the placement of the eyes on stalks, and the forward-projecting antennae.
